children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Attorney General may establish, consistent with all existing Federal laws relating to the protection of privacy, a National Internet Crimes Against Children Data System. The system shall not be used to search for or obtain any information that does not involve the use of the Internet to facilitate child exploitation.

children.heading: Intent of Congress
children.label: b Intent of Congress
children.text: It is the purpose and intent of Congress that the National Internet Crimes Against Children Data System established in subsection (a) is intended to facilitate online law enforcement investigations of child exploitation, information sharing, and the capacity to collect and aggregate data on the extent of the problems of child exploitation.
Federal agencies investigating and prosecuting child exploitation;
the ICAC Task Force Program established under ;section 21112 of this title
State, local, and tribal agencies investigating and prosecuting child exploitation; and
foreign or international law enforcement agencies, subject to approval by the Attorney General.

children.heading: Cyber safe deconfliction and information sharing
children.label: d Cyber safe deconfliction and information sharing

children.text: shall be housed and maintained within the Department of Justice or a credentialed law enforcement agency;
shall be made available for a nominal charge to support credentialed law enforcement agencies in accordance with subsection (c); and

children.label: A
children.text: allow Federal, State, local, and tribal agencies and ICAC task forces investigating and prosecuting child exploitation to contribute and access data for use in resolving case conflicts;

children.label: B
children.text: provide, directly or in partnership with a credentialed law enforcement agency, a dynamic undercover infrastructure to facilitate online law enforcement investigations of child exploitation;

children.label: C
children.text: facilitate the development of essential software and network capability for law enforcement participants; and

children.label: D
children.text: provide software or direct hosting and support for online investigations of child exploitation activities, or, in the alternative, provide users with a secure connection to an alternative system that provides such capabilities, provided that the system is hosted within a governmental agency or a credentialed law enforcement agency.

children.heading: Collection and reporting of data
children.label: e Collection and reporting of data

children.heading: Real-time reporting
children.label: A Real-time reporting
children.text: All child exploitation cases involving local child victims that are reasonably detectable using available software and data are, immediately upon their detection, made available to participating law enforcement agencies.

children.label: i
children.text: identify high-priority suspects, as such suspects are determined by indicators of seriousness of offense or dangerousness to the community or a potential local victim; and

children.label: ii
children.text: report all such identified high-priority suspects to participating law enforcement agencies.

children.heading: Annual reports
children.label: C Annual reports
children.text: Any statistical data indicating the overall magnitude of child pornography trafficking and child exploitation in the United States and internationally is made available and included in the National Strategy, as is required under .section 21111(c)(16) of this title

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: 2 Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to limit the ability of participating law enforcement agencies to disseminate investigative leads or statistical information in accordance with State and local laws.

a secure, online system for Federal law enforcement agencies, ICAC task forces, and other State, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies for use in resolving case conflicts, as provided in subsection (d);

a secure system enabling online communication and collaboration by Federal law enforcement agencies, ICAC task forces, and other State, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies regarding ongoing investigations, investigatory techniques, best practices, and any other relevant news and professional information;
a secure online data storage and analysis system for use by Federal law enforcement agencies, ICAC task forces, and other State, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies;
secure connections or interaction with State and local law enforcement computer networks, consistent with reasonable and established security protocols and guidelines;
guidelines for use of the National Internet Crimes Against Children Data System by Federal, State, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies and ICAC task forces; and
training and technical assistance on the use of the National Internet Crimes Against Children Data System by Federal, State, local, and tribal law enforcement agencies and ICAC task forces.

children.heading: National Internet Crimes Against Children Data System Steering Committee
children.label: g National Internet Crimes Against Children Data System Steering Committee

children.text: 3 representatives elected by the local directors of the ICAC task forces, such representatives shall represent different geographic regions of the country;
1 representative of the Department of Justice Office of Information Services;
1 representative from the law enforcement agency having primary responsibility for hosting and maintaining the National Internet Crimes Against Children Data System;
1 representative of the Federal Bureau of Investigation’s Innocent Images National Initiative or Regional Computer Forensic Lab program;
1 representative of the Immigration and Customs Enforcement’s Cyber Crimes Center;
1 representative of the United States Postal Inspection Service; and
2 representatives of the Department of Justice.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated for each of the fiscal years 2009 through 2016, $2,000,000 to carry out the provisions of this section.
